pattern: For each cell the largest centred plus is the minimum of its four directional runs of ones, computed by four sweeps of the grid.
hints: The arm length a plus can have at a cell is limited by how far an unbroken line of ones extends in each of the four directions.
For every cell compute the consecutive ones reaching up, down, left, and right; the plus order centred there is the smallest of those four counts.
Run four directional passes accumulating run lengths (resetting to zero at a mine), keep the per-cell minimum across passes, then return the maximum such minimum over all cells.

edge_inputs.description: five by five with one mine yields order two
single cell mined leaves no plus
single cell with no mines is order one
empty grid with no mines gives the full center order
mine at the would-be center forces a smaller plus
two by two fully unmined still only fits order one

stress.description: moderate grids with a random scattering of unique mines
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(40, max(1, size)))
    cells = [(x, y) for x in range(n) for y in range(n)]
    rng.shuffle(cells)
    k = rng.randint(0, min(len(cells), n * n // 2))
    mines = [[x, y] for (x, y) in cells[:k]]
    return [n, mines]
stress.note: mines drawn without replacement stay unique and in range
